channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Some Arab Americans who voted for Trump are concerned about his picks for key positions
item.description: Some Arab Americans are expressing concern over Donald Trump's picks for key positions in his next administration.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump taps FCC commissioner Brendan Carr to chair the agency
item.description: Trump said he first nominated Carr to the FCC in 2017 and he was confirmed unanimously by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Just before the election, a poll from Selzer sent shockwaves nationwide after it found Vice President Harris leading President-elect Trump in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Lee threatens to not seat Casey in Senate if he continues refusal to concede
item.description: Sen. Mike Lee, R-Utah, took to social media to threaten that the Senate could refuse to seat Sen. Bob Casey, D-Pa., if he relies on "illegal" ballots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big voter turnout this year benefited Republicans, contradicting conventional political wisdom
item.description: The 2024 presidential election featured sky-high turnout, approaching the historic levels of the 2020 contest and contradicting long-held conventional political wisdom that Republicans struggle to win races in which many people vote.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab Americans in swing state urge Trump to follow through on promises to end conflict in Gaza
item.description: Leaders in Dearborn, Michigan, are calling on President-elect Trump to use his political influence and attempt to bring a swifter end to the conflict in Gaza and Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic AGs rush to form line of defense against Trump
item.description: Democratic attorneys general across the country are readying their legal defenses against the incoming Trump administration, preparing to pounce on potential violations and even take the president-elect to court if he implements controversial policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Pentagon pick paid woman after sex assault allegation but denies wrongdoing, his lawyer says
item.description: The lawyer for Pete Hegseth says that Donald Trump’s pick for defense secretary paid a woman who accused him of sexual assault to head off the threat of a baseless lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump allies remain in legal peril despite his election victory
item.description: President-elect Trump’s White House victory set his legal cases on a downward spiral, but his allies who have faced prosecution or other legal trouble are not yet in the clear.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's sympathetic views toward Russia cause alarm as Trump's pick to lead intelligence services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard, President-elect Donald Trump's choice to lead the U.S. intelligence services, in 2022 endorsed one of Russia's main justifications for invading Ukraine: the existence of dozens of U.S.-funded biolabs working on some of the world's nastiest pathogens.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, ELLEN KNICKMEYER and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking a break from Cabinet picks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Debt-ridden Harris campaign spent $2.6 million on private flights for staff in October: FEC documents
item.description: Kamala Harris' presidential campaign, which is $20 million in debt, spent $2.6 million on private jets in October, according to Federal Election Commission filings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anxiety and dismay inside the Justice Department after Trump taps Gaetz as attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump's choice of Matt Gaetz to be attorney general has many Justice Department employees reeling, worried not only about their own jobs but the future of the agency that the Trump loyalist has railed against.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nicholas Begich projected to win Alaska US House-at-large race
item.description: The top four candidates on the general election ballot included a Democrat serving a 20-year prison sentence in New York.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Iranian official met with Musk in a possible step to ease tensions with Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Iran successfully sought a meeting with Elon Musk, according to a U.S. official, one in a series of steps that appeared aimed at easing tensions with President-elect Donald Trump. Iran's U.N.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Clark County finds 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots in secure vault
item.description: Clark County elections officials discovered more than 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots Thursday, more than a week after Election Day, a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback
item.description: RFK Jr. wants to tackle chronic disease. Despite controversial views on vaccines, his focus on healthy food and taking on special interests may find broad support — and face political headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At a Minneapolis brunch, hundreds of Black women ‘choose joy’ despite Trump win
item.description: Sheletta Brundidge organized a brunch for Black women in Minneapolis on Saturday morning after the election that drew over 200 attendees seeking to unpack and recharge.
item.dc:creator: Feven Gerezgiher, MPR News

item.title: NJ Transit, Amtrak testify before New Jersey lawmakers
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Some palpable tension could be felt during Thursday's Assembly Transportation and Independent Authorities (TIA) Committee, which featured NJ Transit President Kevin Corbett and Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Sen. Smith: Trump cabinet picks are concerning, but I will give each a fair hearing
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Sen. Tina Smith shared she will look closely at each nomination ahead of a hearing. But Trump wants the new Senate leader to allow him to make appointments when the chamber is on recess, bypassing confirmation votes altogether.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Many Californians would back Kamala Harris for California governor, new poll suggests
item.description: It’s unclear what Vice President Kamala Harris will do after losing the presidential election, but should she run for California governor, support among state residents is seemingly strong, new polling suggests.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Thune elected Senate majority leader
item.description: Sen. John Thune (R-S.D.) has been elected Senate majority leader, setting the stage for him to replace retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell (Ky.), who has held the top Senate GOP leadership job for the past 18 years. Thune has served as Senate Republican whip, the No.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tapping Noem to lead Homeland Security: Reports
item.description: Noem was first floated as a possible running mate for Trump, but her consideration fizzled after the release of her biography, in which she described killing a dog that was “untrainable.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expected to choose Sen. Marco Rubio for secretary of state
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to name Sen. Marco Rubio of Florida as secretary of state for his administration in the coming days, according to three sources familiar with the selection process.
item.dc:creator: Dasha Burns, Gabe Gutierrez, Matt Dixon and Zoë Richards, WFLA, NBC
